excerpt.as_written: ... ܕܒܝܬ ܒܢܣܖ̈ܐ ܕܐܪܙܐ ܡܢ ܫܬܐ̈ܣܘܗܝ ܘܥܕܡܐ ܠܫܡܘ̈ܗܝ
ܘܐܪܓܙ ܩܕܡ ܡܪܝܐ ܐܠܗܐ ܕܐܝܪܣܝܠ. ܐܝܟ ܕܥܒ̣ܕ ܐܒܘܗܝ ...

note: Contents: (f. 8r) Thursday of the Mid-Lent and for the renovation of the altar (1 Kings 8:1–); (f. 30r) commemoration of the martyrs (1 Kings 13:1–); (f. 43v) commemoration of the bishops (1 Kings 17:1–); (f. 47v) Wednesday of the Week of Rest and commemoration of Peter and Paul (1 Kings 18:21–); (f. 51v) beginning of the Lent, Tuesday of the Week of Rest, and commemoration of John the Baptist (1 Kings 19:1–); (f. 55r) Friday of the Week of Rest (1 Kings 20:22–); (f. 57v) Saturday of the Week of Rest and commemoration of saint Stephen (1 Kings 21:1–); (f. 65v) commemoration of the bishops (1 Kings 22:52–); (f. 68r) Ascension, Baptism and the Midnight office of Easter (2 Kings 2:1–); (f. 74r) first Saturday of the Lent and commemoration of the diseased (2 Kings 4:8–); (f. 77v) Nativity and the Ninth Hour of the Great Friday (2 Kings 5:1–); (f. 80v) Friday of the Week of Rest (2 Kings 6:1–); (f. 81r) commemoration of the Apostles (2 Kings 6:8–);

bib.citation: Gottlieb, Hans, in collaboration with E. Hammershaimb, eds. The Old Testament in Syriac according to the Peshitta Version. Part II, fasc. 4: The Book of Kings. Leiden: Brill, 1976
